title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinoides
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinoides, also known as a subspecies of the Rough Hawk's-beard, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, thanks to its astringent and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinoides include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
